# What’s the Best Soil for Growing Cannabis?
_Last updated Feb 14, 2026_
by Nebula Haze
If you’re here to learn what soil grows the best weed, you’ve come to the right place. I’ll show you how to choose soil that makes it easy to grow great marijuana.
My favorite cannabis soil is Fox Farm [Coco Loco](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FBA_FX14100-Potting-Cubic/dp/B00HP30LWW?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=3b4855534dcd0f15594c67b278cd53a6&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl). Works well with [Fox Farm Soil Trio](https://www.amazon.com/FoxFarm-Liquid-Nutrient-Trio-Formula/dp/B07TH2FMP3?crid=35B7TN286D0DX&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.DphxsblqxTsaKyiFoL_w-ata68YNXPRU67pD6-1bxoUbT2AW30Q-JGdyiigyCzNJ4ees_4iJUDZQ6xMl3vHyMejP6aD14sEsCsg3FZmeMQ8LjiGpKN0huI9U6agGtR5L7qHodO6efzcJEX-rA3InqOM9W1xIar0Qq7AkK3BljGiz1FLf6Oo59D9KoNf6RezequciMb4tmbgk27P0SyNT7e47AY2XG0fJtrVihtoF0m3UXOhF4nIDpIg18_TQdnQBRDVAmgz4V62TgWYv1RiCSYWch_07ba0eIRQKQD5tGfo.fkHFfRpiseghTCu78FOY0Qr6Uj2iJ21RgokcWT7-gl0&dib_tag=se&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Bnutrients&qid=1764991493&sprefix=fox%2Bfarm%2Bnutrient%2Caps%2C171&sr=8-9&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=565d1cbec89dd2ff15a33d020dd828a4&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) nutrients.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/fox-farm-coco-loco-soil-nutrients-sweet-dreams-grow-journal-growweedeasy.jpg)
When it comes to growing cannabis in soil, you have 3 main choices to ensure great weed.
  1. **Buy a proven soil** – Use a brand known for great cannabis soil. Two great cannabis soil options are [Coco Loco potting mix](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FBA_FX14100-Potting-Cubic/dp/B00HP30LWW?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=3b4855534dcd0f15594c67b278cd53a6&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) (a coco fiber heavy mix makes seedlings less prone to overwatering, but needs nutrients earlier) and [Ocean Forest soil](https://www.amazon.com/FOXFARM-FX14053-Forest-Garden-Potting/dp/B00HXOI3U0?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=3b858169b339817cbad1ab9da0c16897&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) (true soil that is”hot” enough you don’t need to add nutrients for several weeks).
  2. **Use locally available soil** – Go to a local gardening store and find “organic potting mix” that looks like the soil on this page. Cannabis soil should be brown, rich, fluffy, and ideally already contains little white rocks called [perlite](https://www.amazon.com/Riare-Quarts-Organic-Perlite-Plants/dp/B0CGTG24T4?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=d842ba28d762303c4dd74b5bfe2d869f&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl). Make sure to give nutrients after just a few weeks, as cannabis plants quickly use up nutrients in regular soil.
  3. **Mix your own soil** – If you’re looking to make your own soil, [here’s an awesome tutorial by Subcool](https://www.growweedeasy.com/organic-super-soil#make-super-soil) on making cannabis-friendly soil.

Example of perfect cannabis soil – click for closeup
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/example-of-rich-beautiful-cannabis-soil.jpg)
_Note about soil & nutrients:_ Cannabis plants are exceptionally nutrient-hungry as they enter the flowering stage. Bigger plants often use up nutrients in the soil, and need nutrient supplementation right as buds are forming, unless you grow in a big pot with a lot of soil. [Learn about the best cannabis soil nutrients](https://www.growweedeasy.com/best-soil-nutrients-for-cannabis).
Cannabis specifically needs plenty of Phosphorus, Potassium, and micronutrients (a “bloom” nutrient formula) to grow big, dense, potent, and strong-smelling buds.
Give extra “bloom” nutrients as buds form to increase potency, density, and smell.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/watering-cannabis-plants.jpg)
Examples of great fertilizers (nutrients) for cannabis in soil include the [Fox Farm soil trio](https://www.amazon.com/FoxFarm-Liquid-Nutrient-Trio-Formula/dp/B07TH2FMP3?crid=35B7TN286D0DX&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.DphxsblqxTsaKyiFoL_w-ata68YNXPRU67pD6-1bxoUbT2AW30Q-JGdyiigyCzNJ4ees_4iJUDZQ6xMl3vHyMejP6aD14sEsCsg3FZmeMQ8LjiGpKN0huI9U6agGtR5L7qHodO6efzcJEX-rA3InqOM9W1xIar0Qq7AkK3BljGiz1FLf6Oo59D9KoNf6RezequciMb4tmbgk27P0SyNT7e47AY2XG0fJtrVihtoF0m3UXOhF4nIDpIg18_TQdnQBRDVAmgz4V62TgWYv1RiCSYWch_07ba0eIRQKQD5tGfo.fkHFfRpiseghTCu78FOY0Qr6Uj2iJ21RgokcWT7-gl0&dib_tag=se&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Bnutrients&qid=1764991493&sprefix=fox%2Bfarm%2Bnutrient%2Caps%2C171&sr=8-9&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=565d1cbec89dd2ff15a33d020dd828a4&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) or Dyna-Gro [Grow + Bloom](https://www.amazon.com/Dyna-Gro-DYNAGB8OZSET-Liquid-Grow-Bloom/dp/B014UAEPVK?&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=503b8d04c9f9faa86e1a23c86429cbfc&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl). Some growers opt to add [super soil concentrate](https://www.amazon.com/NATURES-LIVING-SOIL-Organic-Concentrate/dp/B07BDKTT7Q?&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=bed2decc5b0d8fcebd13850c6ee5420b&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) to soil directly, instead of regular nutrients.
Weed grown in [Coco Loco soil](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FBA_FX14100-Potting-Cubic/dp/B00HP30LWW?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=3b4855534dcd0f15594c67b278cd53a6&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) with [Fox Farm soil trio](https://www.amazon.com/FoxFarm-Liquid-Nutrient-Trio-Formula/dp/B07TH2FMP3?crid=35B7TN286D0DX&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.DphxsblqxTsaKyiFoL_w-ata68YNXPRU67pD6-1bxoUbT2AW30Q-JGdyiigyCzNJ4ees_4iJUDZQ6xMl3vHyMejP6aD14sEsCsg3FZmeMQ8LjiGpKN0huI9U6agGtR5L7qHodO6efzcJEX-rA3InqOM9W1xIar0Qq7AkK3BljGiz1FLf6Oo59D9KoNf6RezequciMb4tmbgk27P0SyNT7e47AY2XG0fJtrVihtoF0m3UXOhF4nIDpIg18_TQdnQBRDVAmgz4V62TgWYv1RiCSYWch_07ba0eIRQKQD5tGfo.fkHFfRpiseghTCu78FOY0Qr6Uj2iJ21RgokcWT7-gl0&dib_tag=se&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Bnutrients&qid=1764991493&sprefix=fox%2Bfarm%2Bnutrient%2Caps%2C171&sr=8-9&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=565d1cbec89dd2ff15a33d020dd828a4&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) nutrients ([Candy Games #38](https://seedsupreme.com/candy-games-38-feminized-cannabis-seeds.html#grower) strain).
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/flowering-day-67-candy-games-38-trichomes-bud-closeup-growweedeasy.jpg)
**What should you look for in good cannabis soil?**
Weed growers consistently find the best cannabis soil has these characteristics:
  * **dark and rich**
  * **loose texture**
  * **holds water**
  * **drains well**

The following video shows the soil texture you want (this is [Coco Loco soil](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FBA_FX14100-Potting-Cubic/dp/B00HP30LWW/ref=as_li_ss_tl?dchild=1&keywords=fox+farm+coco+loco&qid=1587582828&sr=8-1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=202c64f14cae8d9fd0ad475cd05b8fdd&language=en_US), my personal favorite soil for growing cannabis).
Video Player
<https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/04/example-great-soil-for-growing-cannabis.mp4>
00:00
00:00
00:05
[Use Up/Down Arrow keys to increase or decrease volume.](javascript:void\(0\);)
The little white rocks are called [perlite](https://www.amazon.com/Espoma-PR8-8-Quart-Organic-Perlite/dp/B002Y0AK6S/ref=as_li_ss_tl?dchild=1&keywords=perlite&qid=1587584605&s=lawn-garden&sr=1-2&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=a1d6240e444c23fd3f164b980a4dd8a0&language=en_US), which loosens the soil. Perlite also creates air pockets that help cannabis roots get access to more oxygen. The best cannabis soil nearly always contains some perlite.
Basically you want soil that holds nutrients, air, and water without getting muddy (the goal is wet soil, not dirt batter!).
Example of BAD cannabis soil (also known as soaking wet “dirt batter”)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/damping-off-bad-soil.jpg)
But beyond that, what do you look for to ensure you have happy, healthy cannabis plants?
Some growers choose to use an amended [“hot” soil that slowly releases nutrients over time](https://www.growweedeasy.com/how-to-grow-organic-weed-the-easy-way). With this type of soil, you typically add water or natural supplements like worm tea from seed to harvest. Other growers prefer a lighter potting mix so they have more control and give nutrients in the water once the plant roots have used up the nutrients in the soil. But which cannabis soil brands can you trust?
A little [Mandarin Cookies](https://www.seedsman.com/us-en/mandarin-cookies-v2-feminized-seeds-etho-macov2-fem?a_aid=536dc52ef2d64) seedling getting transplanted into good soil.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/transplanting-cannabis-seedling-to-fresh-soil-growweedeasy-grow-journal.jpg)
Some popular soil examples that I’ve used with good results include:
**[Fox Farm Coco Loco soil](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FBA_FX14100-Potting-Cubic/dp/B00HP30LWW/ref=as_li_ss_tl?dchild=1&keywords=fox+farm+coco+loco&qid=1587582828&sr=8-1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=202c64f14cae8d9fd0ad475cd05b8fdd&language=en_US)**
A coco-based soil mix with enough nutrients to last your plants for a few weeks. With Coco Loco, you should start supplementing with extra nutrients once plants are 2-3 weeks old. I personally like Coco Loco the best of any soil mix I’ve used. You can use it by itself and it’s also my favorite base potting mix for a [“just add water” super soil grow](https://www.growweedeasy.com/just-add-water-super-soil-grow-guide-easy-organic-weed) (more info below).
Seedlings are more resistant in a coco-based soil medium. Cannabis plants in Coco Loco tend to grow happy and healthy while being more resistant to over or under-watering compared to the other soil mixes I’ve tried. It’s great soil for other types of crops too.
_Coco Loco Soil Tips:_
  * **Give nutrients after 2-3 weeks** – Start giving very small doses of nutrients in the water with something like [Fox Farm soil trio](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FX14049-Nutrient-Formula/dp/B00572026S?dchild=1&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Btrio%2Bsoil&qid=1587583542&s=lawn-garden&sr=1-3&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=214b3ade7d5488f5edac3d067088faa7&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) once the plants are a few weeks old. This ensures plants grow as fast with green, healthy leaves.

**[Fox Farm Coco Loco soil](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FBA_FX14100-Potting-Cubic/dp/B00HP30LWW/ref=as_li_ss_tl?dchild=1&keywords=fox+farm+coco+loco&qid=1587582828&sr=8-1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=202c64f14cae8d9fd0ad475cd05b8fdd&language=en_US) **– Fast growing seedlings, with nutrients to last a few weeks.
[](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FBA_FX14100-Potting-Cubic/dp/B00HP30LWW/ref=as_li_ss_tl?dchild=1&keywords=fox+farm+coco+loco&qid=1587582828&sr=8-1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=202c64f14cae8d9fd0ad475cd05b8fdd&language=en_US)
Seedlings in Coco Loco are harder to overwater than many other soils.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/autoflowering-marijuana-seedlings-just-transplanted-to-3-gallon-pots-full-coco-loco-soil.jpg)
Those 6 autoflowering plants at harvest. [Check out the grow journal](https://www.growweedeasy.com/nebulas-auto-flowering-cannabis-grow-journal).
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/autoflowering-cannabis-plants-harvest-the-gang-is-all-here.jpg)
**[Fox Farm Ocean Forest soil](https://www.amazon.com/FOXFARM-FX14053-Forest-Garden-Potting/dp/B00HXOI3U0?dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.NY53hI5ktdalxczC88WsjRAVRZtL3vbpjtYiqw2NfUpR-6BjGYarHzJTQvdBP06ccC6F2nI_95rbNzHrd9LKoUQLKqctDPq4lFrwAGmGeZSaMJGrvTvzfuVT9Yf6DcT1aIv0pFn29jt9mpRAaRMz9fDBBcNdBDthKL0sbs9BxgSFumh3-iWtMF-BCBNBVFiNqr1qzm70GyQ9JnjiPuxOOhBMW_-Jhf6YGFdzr7oGMm8QBnzrOZdBrgj1CawV5LqCkajQhAASlT_IN3x2GF0nsy9Za5U-_dJ6ePeuMoYuTxM.E0AuO3HsRhq_4t4VYqZqd45WkNmzw_qHaHUuXzQPy0c&dib_tag=se&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Bocean%2Bforest&qid=1764992460&sr=8-1&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=bd6ef9c5cf29b5f217de786ea746156e&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) **
A “hot” soil mix with lots of nutrients packed inside. You can start seedlings directly in this mix though they may show signs of [nutrient burn](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-plant-problems/nutrient-burn) at first until they get adjusted.
Ocean Forest has enough nutrients to last your plants quite a while, though you likely should still give extra flowering nutrients once your plants start making buds in order to get the best yields, density, and bud quality. Cannabis plants need a surprisingly lot of nutrients in the flowering stage and you don’t want to starve the plants right as buds are forming.
_Ocean Forest Soil Tips:_
  * **Give nutrients after 4 weeks** – Start giving very small doses of nutrients in the water with something like [Fox Farm soil trio](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FX14049-Nutrient-Formula/dp/B00572026S?dchild=1&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Btrio%2Bsoil&qid=1587583542&s=lawn-garden&sr=1-3&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=214b3ade7d5488f5edac3d067088faa7&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) once the plants are 3-4 weeks old. This ensures plants grow as fast with green, healthy leaves.

**[Fox Farm Ocean Forest soil](https://www.amazon.com/FOXFARM-FX14053-Forest-Garden-Potting/dp/B00HXOI3U0?dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.NY53hI5ktdalxczC88WsjRAVRZtL3vbpjtYiqw2NfUpR-6BjGYarHzJTQvdBP06ccC6F2nI_95rbNzHrd9LKoUQLKqctDPq4lFrwAGmGeZSaMJGrvTvzfuVT9Yf6DcT1aIv0pFn29jt9mpRAaRMz9fDBBcNdBDthKL0sbs9BxgSFumh3-iWtMF-BCBNBVFiNqr1qzm70GyQ9JnjiPuxOOhBMW_-Jhf6YGFdzr7oGMm8QBnzrOZdBrgj1CawV5LqCkajQhAASlT_IN3x2GF0nsy9Za5U-_dJ6ePeuMoYuTxM.E0AuO3HsRhq_4t4VYqZqd45WkNmzw_qHaHUuXzQPy0c&dib_tag=se&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Bocean%2Bforest&qid=1764992460&sr=8-1&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=bd6ef9c5cf29b5f217de786ea746156e&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) **– A “hot” soil mix that can burn seedlings, but provides nutrients for several weeks.
[](https://www.amazon.com/FOXFARM-FX14053-Forest-Garden-Potting/dp/B00HXOI3U0?dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.NY53hI5ktdalxczC88WsjRAVRZtL3vbpjtYiqw2NfUpR-6BjGYarHzJTQvdBP06ccC6F2nI_95rbNzHrd9LKoUQLKqctDPq4lFrwAGmGeZSaMJGrvTvzfuVT9Yf6DcT1aIv0pFn29jt9mpRAaRMz9fDBBcNdBDthKL0sbs9BxgSFumh3-iWtMF-BCBNBVFiNqr1qzm70GyQ9JnjiPuxOOhBMW_-Jhf6YGFdzr7oGMm8QBnzrOZdBrgj1CawV5LqCkajQhAASlT_IN3x2GF0nsy9Za5U-_dJ6ePeuMoYuTxM.E0AuO3HsRhq_4t4VYqZqd45WkNmzw_qHaHUuXzQPy0c&dib_tag=se&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Bocean%2Bforest&qid=1764992460&sr=8-1&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=bd6ef9c5cf29b5f217de786ea746156e&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
A seedling might get a touch of nutrient burn at first, but quickly grows out of it. (pic by NicoleNoel)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/slightly-burnt-seedling-ocean-forest-soil-by-NicoleNoel-sm.jpg)
The good thing is a cannabis plant in Ocean Forest soil won’t need extra nutrients for up to 4 weeks.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/cannabis-plant-fox-farm-ocean-forest-soil.jpg)
[**“Just Add Water” Super Soil**](https://www.growweedeasy.com/just-add-water-super-soil-grow-guide-easy-organic-weed)
Basically you add [concentrated compost](https://www.amazon.com/NATURES-LIVING-SOIL-Organic-Concentrate/dp/B07BDKTT7Q?crid=29WYLV36A6HDU&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.pxmoL082W945C5ct0hdbNe3JGPyENhxs3x5k_ims8LgtGyi6Oa3TGq2k3HX5ZRunY4vq6_aA3snuUQm7gQONlNYAgpqUSiuIl0W6ysp6EA9Dl_1vIpxTzgdlHeqQA4N058GJADNUOqM3C4qi3slfP6FUgDGrFMxEHVl8idUSoHvZuJzPco9awQ__N3PLHh9Qw-vkz2X6BsBBoK6D6QJxg0GhYrBfUul_kKaf67Fem4L5KS8igWPk-7BNfmMUiGFaA8PW9ebV5r03W2agE6pTvlM0F8lSgA_O-WzfzBv1IZo.S2UsUOxNMb0_1nRPpBa7Go9zBXbZr96nJVBUbMkFpeI&dib_tag=se&keywords=natures+super+soil&qid=1765046853&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=natures+super+soil%2Clawngarden%2C141&sr=1-5&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=aa0cbf6027178c656bbd3bdf1bad2890&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) to supercharge your soil with extra slow-release organic amendments. Mix the concentrated compost with regular soil and put the concentrated soil at the bottom 1/3 of a pot. Then fill the rest of the way with regular soil.
The roots won’t get burned at first but can “dip into” the nutrients at the bottom of the pot as needed, like a reservoir of nutrients. This lets the plant go through the nutrients slowly over the course of the grow.
As a result, you can give just plain water without nutrients until harvest.
_Super Soil Tips:_
  * **Avoid runoff water** – Extra water that runs out of the bottom of the pot washes away nutrients. You want to give just enough water for roots to be happy, but not much more. Here’s a proven 30-day [seedling watering schedule](https://www.growweedeasy.com/watering-schedule-for-cannabis-seedlings-in-a-3-gallon-or-5-gallon-pot) in a 3-gallon pot or bigger that won’t make runoff.
  * **Give extra nutrients if needed** – Cannabis buds need a lot of nutrients to get big and dense. Even in super soil, plants still might run out of nutrients if they’ve been in the same pot of soil for a while. This is most common with bigger plants in smaller pots with powerful grow lights. If necessary, supplement with extra organic bloom nutrients like organic [Dr. Earth Flower Girl](https://www.amazon.com/Dr-Earth-707P-Organic-Fertilizer/dp/B0047COGFW?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=d81272faa7cf6f1254bb201d3d0fe6a6&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) right as buds start forming (when nutrient needs are highest). This helps ensure plants get all the crucial minerals needed to make buds.

Cannabis plants grown in [super soil](https://www.growweedeasy.com/just-add-water-super-soil-grow-guide-easy-organic-weed).
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/es300-electric-sky-led-grow-light-kind-organic-soil-day-34-flowering-1.jpg)
**A local “organic soil potting mix”**
If you can’t order special soil online, ask for the best soil at your local gardening store.
You can use almost any organic soil potting mix to grow cannabis. I say “organic” because it ensures there is composted matter and cuts out a lot of potentially problematic ingredients like slow-release chemical nutrients (which often cause nutrient issues in the flowering stage by delivering too much Nitrogen).
_Buying Local Soil Tips_
  * **You’re growing “potted herbs”** – If cornered about _what_ you’re growing, don’t say tomatoes. That seems a bit suspicious from someone who’s clearly a total beginner at gardening. Say you want to grow herbs in a pot, like basil, cilantro, mint, etc. These herbs need the same type of soil as cannabis.
  * **Start adding nutrients after 2-3 weeks** – Plan to start adding extra nutrients in the water by the time a plant is a few weeks old as the roots will quickly use up everything in regular soil.
  * **Get soil that looks like the following picture** – Try to look for soil with a rich and dark but loose texture. It’s a good sign if you see little white pebbles mixed in (this is [perlite](https://www.amazon.com/Espoma-PR8-8-Quart-Organic-Perlite/dp/B002Y0AK6S/ref=as_li_ss_tl?dchild=1&keywords=perlite&qid=1587584605&s=lawn-garden&sr=1-2&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=a1d6240e444c23fd3f164b980a4dd8a0&language=en_US), which makes soil drain better). If a soil looks like dirt or mud, it’s no good!

Try to get soil that looks like this.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/fox-farm-ocean-forest-soil.jpg)
**Make Your Own Soil**
If you’re going to be needed a LOT of soil, the cheapest option is often to [make your own](https://www.growweedeasy.com/organic-super-soil).
A strain like [Candy Bubatz XL](https://www.seedsman.com/us-en/candy-bubatz-xl-feminized-seeds-dp-cabu-fem?a_aid=536dc52ef2d64) is designed for enormous yields outdoors.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/huge-outdoor-cannabis-plant-in-super-soil.jpg)
**Recommended soil nutrients:**
  * [**Fox Farm Soil trio**](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FX14049-Nutrient-Formula/dp/B00572026S?dchild=1&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Btrio%2Bsoil&qid=1587583542&s=lawn-garden&sr=1-3&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=214b3ade7d5488f5edac3d067088faa7&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) **(Natural)** – These 3 bottles include everything your plants need from seed to harvest. The FF trio has a lot of organic ingredients, and produces superb weed with any high-quality soil.
  * [**Dyna-Gro Grow + Bloom**](https://www.amazon.com/Dyna-Gro-DYNAGB8OZSET-Liquid-Grow-Bloom/dp/B014UAEPVK?crid=2YJAYG97LJ51E&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.PnSr5XBdl5DG__k2nZd0qUqo5hjKACD_9PgIbO6ahB4J7R0jZAG-D_V-p0rY22C-Iz6XYPf7SkMgAGblcFP__jesY06aLvCkLUR3V2T2N3aEEuAyZctB81FbzADWfiUjy36ZFL_X1r2mEY5tBb3oCV3KjtEd-A4p4IztwJ5bqeQIW2IgrA7hUUQwRglTaoKsXWuV05nXrv7fajSB4FASeEX0dS3mfn51w3xBi4bZw-EDNAP-DQLk37jy2JvOhVUhv0zDlwBPt9fdlcyk3AeEw7i8SEuNi90kgCw7kVgpLMI.x-8hmATW6mXjXnMSqCjdzUADeT_42777dOhUzo2d1ag&dib_tag=se&keywords=dyna+gro+grow+bloom&qid=1764992779&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=dyna+gro+grow+bloom%2Clawngarden%2C131&sr=1-5&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=cf998bc365153962bc37f7ccb07fb2c4&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) **(Cheap)** – Inexpensive but effective. Use Grow at 1 tsp/gallon once plants in soil are a few weeks old. After plants start growing buds, switch to Bloom at 1 tsp/gallon.   * [**Humboldts Secret Starter Kit**](https://www.amazon.com/Humboldts-Secret-Starter-Kit-Nutrient/dp/B06XH4533G?content-id=amzn1.sym.25a4f516-dbbc-4f08-bb35-3db334713997%3Aamzn1.sym.25a4f516-dbbc-4f08-bb35-3db334713997&crid=7V6IJHLTEAM5&cv_ct_cx=cannabis%2Bsoil%2Bnutrients&keywords=cannabis%2Bsoil%2Bnutrients&pd_rd_i=B06XH4533G&pd_rd_r=472daabf-8271-43e4-a7ad-64d1a83ee11c&pd_rd_w=4gBj6&pd_rd_wg=6uFzD&pf_rd_p=25a4f516-dbbc-4f08-bb35-3db334713997&pf_rd_r=CS0THE7FC4TMHSP5599E&qid=1764993043&s=lawn-garden&sbo=RZvfv%2F%2FHxDF%2BO5021pAnSA%3D%3D&sprefix=cannabis%2Bsoil%2Bnutrient%2Clawngarden%2C143&sr=1-2-6024b2a3-78e4-4fed-8fed-e1613be3bcce-spons&aref=BzFf8vcRfM&sp_csd=d2lkZ2V0TmFtZT1zcF9zZWFyY2hfdGhlbWF0aWM&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=3d51a09e701e71bf2088cbb93cd910cf&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) **(Advanced)** – For the “mad scientist” grower who wants to go deep into nutrient science.

Plants started growing faster once roots reached the auto-watering bases.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/ac-infinity-auto-watering-pots-growweedeasy-cannabis-grow-journal.jpg)
I liked that I could go days without watering.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/day-52-top-view-cannabis-grow-journal-growweedeasy.jpg)
## How to prevent nutrient deficiencies in soil 
As long as you’ve followed the above steps: start with great cannabis soil and give extra nutrients if necessary, your plants should have all the minerals they need.
But you might still see nutrient deficiencies. Why? There is one more step to ensure the nutrients are _available_ to plant roots. You need to control the pH.
  * **Give soil-friendly nutrients** – In many soil setups, you need to provide extra nutrients in the water, especially after buds start forming. Otherwise buds won’t reach their full potential.
  * **Check your water pH to stop deficiencies** – Cannabis roots can’t properly absorb nutrients unless the soil is **slightly acidic** (6-7 pH). Even when plants have all the nutrients they need, you still need to ensure the right pH to prevent nutrient deficiencies. As long as you ensure your water is between 6 and 7 pH, it prevents nutrient deficiencies. Learn how to [check and adjust pH](https://www.growweedeasy.com/ph).

See which [nutrient deficiencies are caused by incorrect pH](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-nutrient-deficiencies-ph-picture-guide). (Picture Guide)
When a plant is “hungry” for more nutrients overall, the leaves start getting pale all over. Especially younger plants.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/nitrogen-deficiency-low-nutrients-vs-healthy-green-cannabis-plants.jpg)
For older plants, it the first sign of low nutrients is usually the lowest leaves start turning yellow and falling off.
**Needs nutrients** – Lower leaves turn yellow, then dry up, then fall on their own.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/10/nitrogen-deficiency-cannabis.jpg)
**Needs nutrients** – Leaves start turning yellow at the bottom first.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/lower-leaves-yellow-wilting-needs-nutrients.jpg)
**Needs nutrients** – Lower leaves turn yellow (or brown if they shrivel up) and fall off on their own
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2010/10/nitrogen-deficiency-yellow-leaves-piled.jpg)
Some deficiencies are actually caused by incorrect pH at the roots. Luckily, it’s an easy fix. [Learn how to tell the difference](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-nutrient-deficiencies-ph-picture-guide) and fix it.
If yellow leaves are at the _top_ of the plant, don’t easily pull off, or come with brown spots, it’s often [a pH problem](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-nutrient-deficiencies-ph-picture-guide) or [light stress](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-plant-problems/light-burn).
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/11/low-ph-nutrient-deficiency.jpg)
Here’s a few more common leaf symptoms caused by [a pH problem](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-nutrient-deficiencies-ph-picture-guide).
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/11/phosphorus-deficiency-caused-by-high-ph-at-the-roots.jpg)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2010/06/copper-deficiency-yellow-tips-leaves.jpg)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2010/10/potassium-deficiency-marijuana-brown-tips.jpg)
[See more pictures of pH-caused nutrient deficiencies](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-nutrient-deficiencies-ph-picture-guide).
## **Tell Me More About Soil for Cannabis: Considerations**
  * Texture
  * Drainage Ability
  * Water Retention

Although that list looks vague and complicated at the same time, the marijuana plant requirements you want to meet are actually pretty simple; let me break it down!
These plants were grown in [Coco Loco](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FBA_FX14100-Potting-Cubic/dp/B00HP30LWW?crid=1URDSUFEJQBT7&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.dFp6Ua3_MI327mzq8dwyOnk9PLJNB2JTxf6zohYHRXEQ-DbRWTI7Q0wDCefOTHd7DiCiiYEMmBPz8I7RVhJjAvUY77KFjXHg-pNYh9vkt1YlIgIhaRQCpXD5Y4RzTbwBWKwOVRtMUkKnm0KhOkqwK0CYazuI6tHo-7MdiMO26zttYue1UKQv8s4a-VgYA1VKBi8iFYpVPPNIm8pd3YbyPj17F6egUMRzhnI57DsO7cX_3Un3f61i37IvJhVLJYy_2R6x04YvN0hItxWLkQfDkyLVsNlhkU0LYWJ-KtbCPR0.iLOOZxDElKzwld3CDop8Dwd0uJV4RE8_qOnTcwgLdSQ&dib_tag=se&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Bsoil&qid=1764991170&sprefix=fox%2Bfarm%2Bsoil%2Caps%2C169&sr=8-1&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=1d1841398c78b2cdd316d8cc56b2460a&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) soil with [Fox Farm soil nutrients](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FX14049-Nutrient-Formula/dp/B00572026S?dchild=1&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Btrio%2Bsoil&qid=1587583542&s=lawn-garden&sr=1-3&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=214b3ade7d5488f5edac3d067088faa7&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl).
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/cannabis-natural-light-flowering-day-44-growweedeasy-grow-journal.jpg)
## Texture, Drainage & Water Retention
It’s easy to get caught up thinking about what nutrients and amendments are in the soil, and those are important, but perhaps the  _most_ important aspect of any soil is actually its texture, ability to drain, and overall water “holding” ability.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/watering-newly-transplanted-cannabis-seedlings-growweedeasy-grow-journal.jpg)
For a cannabis plant to grow and thrive, it needs a good mix of both water and oxygen at the roots at all times! Too much water and the plant roots can’t get enough oxygen (lack of oxygen at the roots is why plants get droopy from [overwatering](https://www.growweedeasy.com/overwatering)), but on the flip side, if there’s not enough water retention, the roots can be injured from drying out too quickly,
Good cannabis soil looks like this.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/espona-organic-potting-mix-soil.jpg)
What gets the best results for growing cannabis is a soil mix with a light texture that is good at retaining water…but not too much.
## **Signs of the Best Cannabis Soil**
  * Appears dark and rich
  * Loose texture
  * Drains well (doesn’t make a pool on top of your soil for more than a couple of seconds and doesn’t take forever to drain out the bottom)
  * Holds water without getting muddy (you want wet soil, not dirt-batter)

[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/rich-soil-perlite-for-growing-cannabis.jpg)
## **Example of “Good” Cannabis Soil Ingredients**
If you have a local gardening or hydro store, they will have most of these ingredients, and cheaper than online.
  * [Composted forest humus](https://www.amazon.com/Fishnure-Conditioning-Fertilizer-Increased-Retention/dp/B086KXT5TQ?crid=1IRXDFU97M3JK&keywords=forest+humus&qid=1686190552&sprefix=forest+humus%2Caps%2C149&sr=8-1-spons&psc=1&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUFRREtFU0JKRkNNSDkmZW5jcnlwdGVkSWQ9QTA1NjY5NzkxNkFUUzhFS0Y3V0pDJmVuY3J5cHRlZEFkSWQ9QTA2MzExOTMxNzRaNFVXUlI5QlRNJndpZGdldE5hbWU9c3BfYXRmJmFjdGlvbj1jbGlja1JlZGlyZWN0JmRvTm90TG9nQ2xpY2s9dHJ1ZQ%3D%3D&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=9d198994540f8280c147f1e2e559d4f4&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
  * Sandy loam
  * [Sphagnum peat moss](https://www.amazon.com/Hoffman-15503-Canadian-Sphagnum-Quarts/dp/B0000CBITW?ie=UTF8&qid=1494291523&sr=8-1&keywords=Sphagnum+peat+moss&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=8cf767f70cdfd5d2af0d3f4ab578caf2&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
  * [Coco coir](https://www.amazon.com/Happy-Trees-Seed-Starting-Triple-Washed-Peat-Free/dp/B0DLLH355B?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=30a03ff592d67a9cb4de35e8c07313f1&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) (sometimes labeled coco fiber)
  * [Perlite](https://www.amazon.com/Espoma-PR8-8-Quart-Organic-Perlite/dp/B002Y0AK6S?s=lawn-garden&ie=UTF8&qid=1494291607&sr=1-2&keywords=perlite&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=470ca16fece0d014faa81ef02b67f3cd&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
  * [Earthworm castings](https://www.amazon.com/Unco-Industries-Earthworm-Castings-Fertilizer/dp/B00062KQ42?s=lawn-garden&ie=UTF8&qid=1494291639&sr=1-1&keywords=Earthworm+castings&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=e60e613a4ae8e8d8c6fcd618fe7d3c25&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
  * [Bat guano](https://www.amazon.com/4lbs-Cavern-CultureTM-Organic-Fertilizer/dp/B07NPRGP1Z?keywords=Bat+guano+fox+farm&qid=1638244028&s=lawn-garden&sr=1-6&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=775258bed82aba73b18641679b01f3ec&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
  * [Fish meal](https://www.amazon.com/Down-Earth-Fish-Meal-Fertilizer/dp/B00F9FVON8?s=lawn-garden&ie=UTF8&qid=1494291693&sr=1-2&keywords=Fish+meal&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=948a03fbcbee3ad91cd28b77a5bdbb95&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
  * [Crab meal](https://www.amazon.com/Neptunes-Harvest-CS604-Shell-Multi-Purpose/dp/B006GK5ON6?dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.yD3erRmMGc7FT2eZ0kHCO2X9uierybTJxJW2QaPqPLcwryZxlr4690fXIrGS1LJes3FisQe6I8vA7QkSWGjx9jSFNiKqh37XPfVub2ZoSMudNe_09ZyTqDbMWL-zZuihf-TrdTIrIyviJM5h_eUQwDEGaz--DYBKj6-JwxbPEa2mqbaeB_L5kYGc51owT24E-FSpfUcwxBKF1C4NjmWvCAziCaLCm67G4MqzpVLjeZFC9VOArjEW9VvZFLjO0hMVPUXl9ikZMgBFxQCBWWBtI1wOf1xxH9s02kBh4RDkWSs.aV-lKlj1x0WlDDQVqfE8Dn_FbDJoKS_1P0MVuy65gfg&dib_tag=se&keywords=Crab%2Bmeal&qid=1764993617&s=lawn-garden&sr=1-4&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=7ce95d8fe6fadf3f7b5998e71c4a3b4f&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
  * [Bone meal](https://www.amazon.com/Burpee-99951-Organic-Bone-Fertilizer/dp/B078GZ4P2B?&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=97556c01f22c599f6c13bb8699895343&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)

  * [Mycorrhizae](https://www.amazon.com/Big-Foot-Mycorrhizal-Mycorrhizae-Intaracides/dp/B01MQTY2Z3?pd_rd_i=B01MQTY2Z3&psc=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=1d0db7ccc39cbab3cd83863b12b98ce0&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
  * [Leonardite](https://www.amazon.com/10-Lb-Pulverized-Leonardite-Unaltered/dp/B01J8XFUWM?s=lawn-garden&ie=UTF8&qid=1494292186&sr=1-5&keywords=Leonardite&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=4f69a9990460c748739c6a038034e4d9&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)

_Note:_ You’ll likely never see any soil mix with ALL those ingredients, but I wanted to share examples of common cannabis-friendly ingredients and amendments that often appear on the label of good soil 🙂
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/mixing-organic-super-soil-marijuana.jpg)
If you are the kind of cannabis grower who’s getting deep into soil and amendments, it’s crazy how much knowledge you can get from visiting any local garden shops and ask the workers what works for them. If you’re not sure about the worker being cannabis-friendly, ask for good soil for “potted herbs” like basil or mint.
If you get the soil part right, you have almost everything you need to get to harvest! With the correct texture, drainage and water retention, you’ve got a perfect base. Add good soil cannabis [nutrients](https://www.growweedeasy.com/nutrients), especially in the budding phase, and you should get to harvest with great results!
Example of happy marijuana plants in good soil just before harvest.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/feminized-blue-dream-plants-harvest-seed-supreme.jpg)
## **More About Common Amendments to Alter Texture, Drainage & Water Retention of Soil**
**Perlite – You should have perlite in your soil**
  * [Perlite](https://www.amazon.com/Espoma-PR8-8-Quart-Organic-Perlite/dp/B002Y0AK6S?s=lawn-garden&ie=UTF8&qid=1478403697&sr=1-1&keywords=perlite&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=356de086df517fb598c0d98f9190f4fd&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) is one of the most common soil amendments. It is highly recommended for any soil mix that doesn’t have some already.
  * Very light, airy white “rocks” that feel almost like popcorn and add oxygen while increasing overall drainage ability.
  * Add perlite to the mix (10-40% of the total volume). Use less perlite if you want better water retention and don’t plan on using a lot of extra nutrients. This is because a lot of extra perlite can cause the nutrients leach out faster from the soil. Add higher levels of perlite if you want to use a lot of added nutrients or supplements without burning your plants (since perlite helps prevent nutrient buildup).

Perlite has a light crunchy texture, almost like giant Pop Rocks.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/08/schultz-horticultural-perlite-growing-weed.jpg)
Perlite looks like little rocks when mixed in the soil
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/fox-farm-ocean-forest-soil.jpg)
**Coco Coir – Cannabis roots love coco coir! (more than peat moss, which is the main alternative)**
  * [Coco coir](https://www.amazon.com/Happy-Trees-Seed-Starting-Triple-Washed-Peat-Free/dp/B0DLLH355B?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=caf806526aeb4661174c7945f75222ac&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) is made from coconut husks and has some unique properties that make it a good amendment for cannabis soil mixtures.
  * Coco improves water retention, but doesn’t make soil heavy.
  * Roots tend to develop faster and plants are less likely to suffer from overwatering in coco coir.
  * Some growers grow in pure coco, but if you’re adding it to a soil mix as an amendment, you might add 10-30% coco coir.

[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/09/coco-coir-brick-breaking-apart.jpg)
Where to get coco?
  * Pre-Mixed – You can buy soil that is already mixed with coco like [Coco Loco](https://www.amazon.com/FoxFarm-Coco-Loco-Potting-Cubic/dp/B00HP30LWW?s=lawn-garden&ie=UTF8&qid=1478404972&sr=1-2&keywords=coco+loco&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=9e1b162bd950d801fff440375b098c47&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl). Personally, I’ve found my cannabis plants love this stuff.
  * Loose Coco – If you want to amend your current soil with coco coir, you can purchase [loose coco coir](https://www.amazon.com/Happy-Trees-Seed-Starting-Triple-Washed-Peat-Free/dp/B0DLLH355B?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=30a03ff592d67a9cb4de35e8c07313f1&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl).
  * Coco Bricks – Your other option is to get [coco bricks](https://www.amazon.com/Plantonix-Coco-listed-Organic-Bricks/dp/B01N1YP8O6?pd_rd_i=B01N1YP8O6&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=e73532665b52d9e82d2e289a75a9972d&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl). One downside is they need to be rehydrated with a bucket and some water, but it’s easy ([learn how to re-hydrate coco bricks](https://www.growweedeasy.com/coco-coir#coco-coir-tutorial-what-you-need)). I like these bricks not only because they are the cheapest option, but also because they’re compact and easy to store.

Coco bricks are compressed, expanding into a soil-like consistency when you add water.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/pure-coco-coir-adding-water.jpg)
**Worm Castings**
  * [Worm castings](https://www.amazon.com/Unco-Industries-Earthworm-Fertilizer-30-Pound/dp/B00V4RI88Y?s=lawn-garden&ie=UTF8&qid=1478405096&sr=1-2&keywords=worm+castings&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=e6f5d9f7f72ba18b1adf5f0c026b7948&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) are a nice term for “worm poop” and cannabis plants _love_ it!
  * Improves texture, drainage, _and_ moisture retention
  * Add a natural source of nutrients that breaks down slowly
  * Usually contains high levels of beneficial micro-organisms due to going through a worm’s digestive system 🙂
  * Add up to 30% worm castings in your soil (although it contains nutrients, it’s gentle enough that it’s unlikely to burn your plants even if you add too much)

Worm castings or “vermicompost” is a nice way to say “worm poop”
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/worms-vermicompost-rich-soil-in-hands-worm-castings-sm.jpg)
You can feed worms and make your own, or buy worm castings in a bag!
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/06/worms-in-compost-soil-in-hand.jpg)
Now here are a few examples of good and bad cannabis soil so you can see the texture you’re looking for!
**Good Cannabis Soil**  
A light, rich soil mix with great drainage. Although there is a wood chip in this picture, for the most part, the mix is completely composted and broken down. It’s normal to see some wood pieces in composted soil, but you don’t want to have to wait for a lot of wood to break down while your plants are growing. You want all that rich nutrient goodness to be readily available to your plant roots 🙂
This is [Fox Farm Ocean Forest soil](https://www.amazon.com/FOXFARM-FX14053-Forest-Garden-Potting/dp/B00HXOI3U0?dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.NY53hI5ktdalxczC88WsjRAVRZtL3vbpjtYiqw2NfUpR-6BjGYarHzJTQvdBP06ccC6F2nI_95rbNzHrd9LKoUQLKqctDPq4lFrwAGmGeZSaMJGrvTvzfuVT9Yf6DcT1aIv0pFn29jt9mpRAaRMz9fDBBcNdBDthKL0sbs9BxgSFumh3-iWtMF-BCBNBVFiNqr1qzm70GyQ9JnjiPuxOOhBMW_-Jhf6YGFdzr7oGMm8QBnzrOZdBrgj1CawV5LqCkajQhAASlT_IN3x2GF0nsy9Za5U-_dJ6ePeuMoYuTxM.E0AuO3HsRhq_4t4VYqZqd45WkNmzw_qHaHUuXzQPy0c&dib_tag=se&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Bocean%2Bforest&qid=1764992460&sr=8-1&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=bd6ef9c5cf29b5f217de786ea746156e&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/11/fox-farm-ocean-forest-soil.jpg)
**Good Cannabis Soil**  
Rich and light **composted** soil. Since this soil doesn’t have a lot of perlite, it’s a good choice for a grower who doesn’t want to add a lot of extra nutrients or supplements in the water.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/11/example-good-cannabis-soil.jpg)
**Good Cannabis Soil**  
This soil has quite a bit of perlite, which is a good choice if you plan to feed heavily with nutrients and supplements since the extra perlite prevents nutrient buildup in the soil.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/11/finished-perlite-coir-mix.jpg)
**Good Cannabis Soil**  
The plant is growing in organic, composted “[super soil](https://www.growweedeasy.com/just-add-water-super-soil-grow-guide-easy-organic-weed)” which has enough amendments to last your entire grow, so the only thing you do is add water!
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/11/happy-green-cannabis-plant-in-organic-super-soil.jpg)
Here’s organic “super” soil up close.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/02/compost-soil.jpg)
**Bad Cannabis Soil**  
This soil is muddy, clumpy and waterlogged. It retains too much moisture, which makes it really easy to overwater your plants.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/11/too-muddy-soil-cannabis-seedling.jpg)
**Bad Cannabis Soil**  
Cannabis soil should not have a whole lot of big visible wood chips in it. That means the soil hasn’t been fully composted, and all the nutrients and goodness in that wood is mostly unavailable to your plants.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/11/bad-cannabis-soil-mulch.jpg)
**Bad Cannabis Soil**  
Although this seedling is over a month old, it has stayed tiny. Its growth is stunted by the thick heavy soil that holds way too much water and not enough air. Note how some of the soil looks like one solid object.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/11/stunted-overpotted-overwatered-small-cannabis-plant.jpg)
**Bad Cannabis Soil**  
Don’t use dirt from outside! It almost never works, especially if it looks like this!
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/11/dirt-from-outside.jpg)
Cannabis seedlings struggle to survive in dry, dusty, pale dirt like this.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/bad-seedling-soil-cannabis-seedlings-dying-bending-sm.jpg)
**Bad Cannabis Soil – Sand!**
I’m sure you probably know by now that sand is certainly not the ideal cannabis soil. But I thought you might be interested to see this submission we received from a reader who tried growing a seedling directly in sand!
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/bad-soil-cannabis-seedling-grown-in-sand.jpg)
# Recap: Suggested Brands for Cannabis Soil
Here’s a quick recap of my recommended options for great cannabis soil.
Soil makes big, beautiful, fragrant buds when used properly.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/nirvana-aurora-indica-cannabis-big-buds-flowering-soil.jpg)
## **Fox Farm – Coco Loco or Ocean Forest Soil**
Fox Farm has been around for over 30 years and makes some of the most common types of “cannabis soil” (at least in the US). They have several great soil mixes, including “Happy Frog” which is a light mix with barely any nutrients, typically only for seedlings and clones.
Their [Ocean Forest soil mix](https://www.amazon.com/FOXFARM-FX14053-Forest-Garden-Potting/dp/B00HXOI3U0?dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.NY53hI5ktdalxczC88WsjRAVRZtL3vbpjtYiqw2NfUpR-6BjGYarHzJTQvdBP06ccC6F2nI_95rbNzHrd9LKoUQLKqctDPq4lFrwAGmGeZSaMJGrvTvzfuVT9Yf6DcT1aIv0pFn29jt9mpRAaRMz9fDBBcNdBDthKL0sbs9BxgSFumh3-iWtMF-BCBNBVFiNqr1qzm70GyQ9JnjiPuxOOhBMW_-Jhf6YGFdzr7oGMm8QBnzrOZdBrgj1CawV5LqCkajQhAASlT_IN3x2GF0nsy9Za5U-_dJ6ePeuMoYuTxM.E0AuO3HsRhq_4t4VYqZqd45WkNmzw_qHaHUuXzQPy0c&dib_tag=se&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Bocean%2Bforest&qid=1764992460&sr=8-1&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=bd6ef9c5cf29b5f217de786ea746156e&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) is “hotter” soil (higher levels of nutrients) that contains ingredients that cannabis plants love, including earthworm castings, bat guano, fish meal and crab meal. The nutrients contained in the soil will provide everything your plant needs for several weeks. Although it might give young seedlings just a touch of [nutrient burn](https://www.growweedeasy.com/nutrient-burn-cannabis) at first, they can be started in Ocean Forest soil and will soon be able to use the nutrients and start growing quickly. Some growers might put a little bit of Happy Frog on top of a container of Ocean Forest, just to make it a little more gentle for seedlings the first week or two.
However, my personal favorite cannabis soil is Fox Farm [Coco Loco soil](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FBA_FX14100-Potting-Cubic/dp/B00HP30LWW?crid=1URDSUFEJQBT7&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.dFp6Ua3_MI327mzq8dwyOnk9PLJNB2JTxf6zohYHRXEQ-DbRWTI7Q0wDCefOTHd7DiCiiYEMmBPz8I7RVhJjAvUY77KFjXHg-pNYh9vkt1YlIgIhaRQCpXD5Y4RzTbwBWKwOVRtMUkKnm0KhOkqwK0CYazuI6tHo-7MdiMO26zttYue1UKQv8s4a-VgYA1VKBi8iFYpVPPNIm8pd3YbyPj17F6egUMRzhnI57DsO7cX_3Un3f61i37IvJhVLJYy_2R6x04YvN0hItxWLkQfDkyLVsNlhkU0LYWJ-KtbCPR0.iLOOZxDElKzwld3CDop8Dwd0uJV4RE8_qOnTcwgLdSQ&dib_tag=se&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Bsoil&qid=1764991170&sprefix=fox%2Bfarm%2Bsoil%2Caps%2C169&sr=8-1&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=1d1841398c78b2cdd316d8cc56b2460a&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl). It is a bit lighter on nutrients so it doesn’t burn young plants, yet still gives them plenty of nutrients for a few weeks. I’ve found that cannabis seems to grow a bit faster and are harder to overwater in coco-based soils vs peat-based soil like Ocean Forest.
Fox Farm also offers a [soil nutrient trio](https://www.amazon.com/FoxFarm-Liquid-Nutrient-Trio-Formula/dp/B07TH2FMP3?crid=35B7TN286D0DX&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.DphxsblqxTsaKyiFoL_w-ata68YNXPRU67pD6-1bxoUbT2AW30Q-JGdyiigyCzNJ4ees_4iJUDZQ6xMl3vHyMejP6aD14sEsCsg3FZmeMQ8LjiGpKN0huI9U6agGtR5L7qHodO6efzcJEX-rA3InqOM9W1xIar0Qq7AkK3BljGiz1FLf6Oo59D9KoNf6RezequciMb4tmbgk27P0SyNT7e47AY2XG0fJtrVihtoF0m3UXOhF4nIDpIg18_TQdnQBRDVAmgz4V62TgWYv1RiCSYWch_07ba0eIRQKQD5tGfo.fkHFfRpiseghTCu78FOY0Qr6Uj2iJ21RgokcWT7-gl0&dib_tag=se&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Bnutrients&qid=1764991493&sprefix=fox%2Bfarm%2Bnutrient%2Caps%2C171&sr=8-9&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=565d1cbec89dd2ff15a33d020dd828a4&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) that are base nutrients (complete nutrition) formulated for plants like cannabis. The soil trio goes great with any of their soils, ensuring plants get the right levels of nutrients for each stage of life.
[Coco Loco soil](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FBA_FX14100-Potting-Cubic/dp/B00HP30LWW?crid=1URDSUFEJQBT7&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.dFp6Ua3_MI327mzq8dwyOnk9PLJNB2JTxf6zohYHRXEQ-DbRWTI7Q0wDCefOTHd7DiCiiYEMmBPz8I7RVhJjAvUY77KFjXHg-pNYh9vkt1YlIgIhaRQCpXD5Y4RzTbwBWKwOVRtMUkKnm0KhOkqwK0CYazuI6tHo-7MdiMO26zttYue1UKQv8s4a-VgYA1VKBi8iFYpVPPNIm8pd3YbyPj17F6egUMRzhnI57DsO7cX_3Un3f61i37IvJhVLJYy_2R6x04YvN0hItxWLkQfDkyLVsNlhkU0LYWJ-KtbCPR0.iLOOZxDElKzwld3CDop8Dwd0uJV4RE8_qOnTcwgLdSQ&dib_tag=se&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Bsoil&qid=1764991170&sprefix=fox%2Bfarm%2Bsoil%2Caps%2C169&sr=8-1&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=1d1841398c78b2cdd316d8cc56b2460a&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) works well with [Fox Farm Soil Trio](https://www.amazon.com/FoxFarm-Liquid-Nutrient-Trio-Formula/dp/B07TH2FMP3?crid=35B7TN286D0DX&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.DphxsblqxTsaKyiFoL_w-ata68YNXPRU67pD6-1bxoUbT2AW30Q-JGdyiigyCzNJ4ees_4iJUDZQ6xMl3vHyMejP6aD14sEsCsg3FZmeMQ8LjiGpKN0huI9U6agGtR5L7qHodO6efzcJEX-rA3InqOM9W1xIar0Qq7AkK3BljGiz1FLf6Oo59D9KoNf6RezequciMb4tmbgk27P0SyNT7e47AY2XG0fJtrVihtoF0m3UXOhF4nIDpIg18_TQdnQBRDVAmgz4V62TgWYv1RiCSYWch_07ba0eIRQKQD5tGfo.fkHFfRpiseghTCu78FOY0Qr6Uj2iJ21RgokcWT7-gl0&dib_tag=se&keywords=fox%2Bfarm%2Bnutrients&qid=1764991493&sprefix=fox%2Bfarm%2Bnutrient%2Caps%2C171&sr=8-9&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=565d1cbec89dd2ff15a33d020dd828a4&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) nutrients.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/fox-farm-coco-loco-soil-nutrients-sweet-dreams-grow-journal-growweedeasy.jpg)
## **“Super” Soil (Living Soil)**
When cannabis growers talk about “super” soil, they’re usually referring to soil that has been _amended_ with slow-releasing organic nutrient sources,_composted_ for several months ([learn more about super soil](https://www.growweedeasy.com/just-add-water-super-soil-grow-guide-easy-organic-weed)).
The composting process creates a “living” soil that is full of microorganisms in the rhizosphere (area around the roots). Properly composted soil has nutrient sources that slowly break down over the course of your plant’s life cycle. It very closely mimics what happens in nature.
Super Soil has a colony of microorganisms living in the soil which form a symbiotic relationship with your plant roots. They deliver nutrients to your plant, and in return they eat the sugars that get secreted by your roots!
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2010/05/organic-soil-marijuana-hand.jpg)
The “micro-herd” in the soil delivers nutrients directly to your plants. As long as you’re using decent water, you usually don’t need to worry about pH or other things that can disrupt nutrient absorption in regular soil.
[Buy Pre-Made Super Soil Compost](https://www.amazon.com/Organic-Super-Soil-Concentrated-Strength/dp/B079TC27PZ?pd_rd_i=B079TC27PZ&psc=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=7acd47a6837f9b3f4c1cf3bc5625a525&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) + a high quality soil and follow the directions to mix them together. After that, just add water!
[Learn more about a “Just Add Water” cannabis soil grow](https://www.growweedeasy.com/just-add-water-super-soil-grow-guide-easy-organic-weed).
However, when growing with Super Soil, it’s a good idea to avoid watering too much at a time, as extra runoff water will drain away some of the nutrients. Try to give just enough water to saturate the soil with very little extra coming out of the bottom. Since you won’t be adding more nutrients through the grow, you want to conserve what’s in the soil!
Nugbuckets is a famous organic soil grower. Check out his plants!
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/09/main-lining-example-marijuana.jpg)[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/09/main-lining-example-grown-out.jpg)
## **Organic Potting Mix**
This is what kind of soil to get if you don’t have any specialized cannabis soil available, but want something that is known to work for growing cannabis.
These cannabis plants grown outdoors in soil by our student Raymond. ([Rainbow Kush Auto](https://www.northatlanticseed.com/product/new-rainbow-kush-auto/ref/63951/) on either end and two [Four-Assed Monkey Autos](https://www.seedsman.com/us-en/4-assed-monkey-auto-feminised-seeds-meph-4ass-auto-fem?a_aid=536dc52ef2d64) in the middle)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/autos-Rainbow-Kush-on-either-end-and-two-Four-Assed-Monkeys-in-the-middle-cannabis-outdoor-plants-raymond-trophy-pic-home-grow-masterclass.jpg)
Generally, anything labeled as an “organic potting mix” will work. This type of mix hasn’t been amended with chemical slow-release nutrients, which is one of the main things you want to avoid with soil for cannabis. I know it sounds like heresy, but even the _Miracle-Gro_ version of “organic potting mix” will work okay, because unlike their original potting mix, it doesn’t contain chemical nutrients (though it still has poor drainage and moisture retention – almost any other type of organic potting mix is better!).
Usually, an organic potting mix does not have enough nutrients to last your plants for more than a few weeks, so it’s a good idea to always supplement with [cannabis-friendly nutrients](https://www.growweedeasy.com/nutrients), especially in the flowering stage when your plant is making buds and needs lots of extra Phosphorus and Potassium.
[Espoma Organic Potting Mix](https://www.amazon.com/Espoma-AP8-8-Quart-Organic-Potting/dp/B002Y04TK6?s=lawn-garden&ie=UTF8&qid=1478400000&sr=1-1&keywords=organic%2Bpotting%2Bmix&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=2a45adfee0176c2bcbe6a82b54d8e226&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) is found in many stores in the US, and works for growing cannabis!
[](https://www.amazon.com/Espoma-AP8-8-Quart-Organic-Potting/dp/B002Y04TK6?s=lawn-garden&ie=UTF8&qid=1478400000&sr=1-1&keywords=organic%2Bpotting%2Bmix&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=cbe09abf5a9b2abe0a54170dca615ef0&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
Example of Espoma organic potting mix
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/espona-organic-potting-mix-soil.jpg)
## **What to Watch Out For With Any Soil Mix At the Store**
  * **Look At and Touch It If You Can!** You already have an idea what soil should look and feel like, but here’s a test: If you form the soil into a ball, it should stick together loosely, but it should also easily fall apart again if you squeeze it.
  * **No “Time Release” Chemical Nutrients in the Soil** – These types of soil slowly release nutrients over the course of months, which provides too much Nitrogen in the flowering stage and could possibly impair overall bud growth.
  * **Soil Should Appear Dark and Rich** – Pale, crumbly or sandy soil usually doesn’t have a lot of nutrient content that the plant roots can get to.

[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/11/fox-farm-ocean-forest-soil.jpg)
  * **Soil Has Little White Rocks In It (Perlite)** , if you see white, almost fluffy rocks dispersed through the soil like popcorn, that is usually a good sign because it means this potting mix was intended to have good drainage.
  * **Soil Isn’t “Heavy”** – Cannabis grows best in soil with a light airy texture and great drainage, which may seem almost fluffy when it’s dry.
  * **Example of “Good” Soil Ingredients** – Composted forest humus, sandy loam, sphagnum peat moss, coco coir (sometimes labeled coco fiber), perlite, earthworm castings, bat guano, fish meal, crab meal, bone meal, blood meal, Azomite, pumice, kelp, dolomite lime, mycorrhizae and leonardite. That’s not everything, just examples of cannabis-friendly ingredients you see the most often 🙂
  * **Examples of “Bad” Soil Ingredients** – You don’t want to see wood or bark on the label if it doesn’t say it’s been composted first. Also if you see just the word “fertilizer” in the ingredients that’s often code for slow-release chemical nutrients, which you don’t want.

Summary: Try to get soil that looks like this for cannabis plants.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/06/hands-in-marijuana-super-soil.jpg)
I hope this soil tutorial helps you find the right soil for your cannabis setup!
